metadata.title: Build a Prompt Anatomy Auditor with Claude

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: A prompt anatomy auditor makes me want to X-ray my own prompts, because I rarely know which parts are actually doing work. I want Claude to break a prompt into its structural components and grade each. Help me: (1) define the anatomy — role, context, task, constraints, output format, examples — and detect which are present or missing; (2) flag ambiguous or contradictory instructions that would degrade the output; (3) have the auditor rewrite the weakest component and show the before-and-after effect on the response.

props.command: A prompt anatomy auditor makes me want to X-ray my own prompts, because I rarely know which parts are actually doing work. I want Claude to break a prompt into its structural components and grade each. Help me: (1) define the anatomy — role, context, task, constraints, output format, examples — and detect which are present or missing; (2) flag ambiguous or contradictory instructions that would degrade the output; (3) have the auditor rewrite the weakest component and show the before-and-after effect on the response.
props.segment: Build a Prompt Anatomy Auditor with

beats.label: Hook — the spec gap
beats.narration: Most prompts fail not because you don't know what you want — but because you've assembled the right information in a structure Claude can't parse. Six components. Most prompts fill two.

shots.notes: Terminal-style text: 'PROMPT ANATOMY AUDIT' header, then two columns: COMPONENT / STATUS. Task: present. Context: missing. Constraints: missing. Format: missing. Examples: missing. Eval Criteria: missing. Crimson Xs on missing rows.

beats.label: Claude command
beats.narration: Here's the seed command. Pipe any prompt into Claude and ask it to map the six anatomy components — present, implied, or missing — with a specificity score for each filled slot.

props.command: claude "Analyze this prompt against the six-component anatomy: Task, Context, Constraints, Output Format, Examples, Evaluation Criteria. For each: present/implied/missing, specificity score 1-5, one-sentence improvement suggestion." < prompt.txt

beats.label: Python code
beats.narration: The Python script reads your prompt, sends it through Claude with the anatomy instruction, then parses the six-component report into a structured output you can inspect.

props.code:
import subprocess, sys
prompt = open(sys.argv[1]).read()
instruction = (
  "Analyze this prompt against the six-component "
  "anatomy: Task, Context, Constraints, Output "
  "Format, Examples, Evaluation Criteria. For each:"
  " present/implied/missing, score 1-5, one-line fix."
)
result = subprocess.run(
  ["claude", instruction],
  input=prompt, capture_output=True, text=True
)
print(result.stdout)

beats.label: Component map
beats.narration: The audit distinguishes implied from missing. Implied means the information is inferable from context. Missing means there is no basis for inference at all. That distinction changes the fix.

shots.notes: Two-column table: COMPONENT / VERDICT. Task: Present (score 4). Context: Implied (score 2). Constraints: Missing (score 0). Format: Missing (score 0). Examples: Missing (score 0). Eval Criteria: Missing (score 0). Color: green=present, gold=implied, crimson=missing.

beats.label: The change — improved prompt
beats.narration: Now run the same auditor on an improved prompt — one that fills the missing slots. Watch the specificity scores move.

props.command: claude "Analyze this prompt against the six-component anatomy..." < prompt_improved.txt

beats.label: Score delta
beats.narration: Before: two components present, average score 1.3. After: six components present, average score 4.1. The bars move. The audit makes the improvement visible.

shots.notes: Side-by-side: BEFORE column (sparse, mostly crimson) vs AFTER column (all six filled, mostly green). Score deltas shown per row. Total score: 8 -> 24 out of 30.

beats.label: Teardown angle
beats.narration: The anatomy is not a template to fill — it's a diagnostic. A prompt missing Evaluation Criteria isn't failing because it's incomplete. It's failing because without criteria, Claude can't know when it's done.

shots.notes: Large text: 'The anatomy is a diagnostic, not a template.' Below: 'Missing: Evaluation Criteria = Claude decides when done. You don't.' Teardown palette — cream background, crimson accent line.

beats.label: Next reel teaser
beats.narration: Next: put the evaluation criteria first — before the task — and watch what changes.

shots.notes: Clean slate: 'NEXT: Criteria-First Generator' with arrow. Minimal teardown style.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Here's what the body just demonstrated.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Build a Prompt Anatomy Auditor with Claude] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
